synthesis_hints: Bias and execution must align with ICT-style context: liquidity, order blocks, FVGs, market structure, sessions as you define them.
Only highlight or favor setups where planned reward:risk falls in the 1:8–1:9 band; reject or downgrade anything below 1:8.

bullish: HTF (weekly/daily) structure supportive of longs — e.g. price holding above key dealing range / bullish breaker context per your ICT rules
bearish: HTF structure supportive of shorts — e.g. rejection from premium / bearish order flow per your ICT rules
neutral: No clear HTF bias or price mid-range / chop — stand aside or reduce size

risk_rules: Reward:risk: only consider trades with planned R:R in the 1:8 to 1:9 range; minimum acceptable R:R is 1:8 (nothing weaker).
Size positions so a full stop-out respects your max risk per trade (define % or $ in your journal).
Maximum 2 correlated positions on the same underlying unless explicitly scaling a planned runner.
Two consecutive full stop-outs: stop trading for the day and review.
No revenge trades after a loss in the same session.
notes: This fork is oriented around ICT-style multi-timeframe analysis (tv ict / tv_ict) and synthesis. Tune ict_report.symbol, timeframes, zones, and drawings (merged before each screenshot) to your watchlist. Use examples/position-tool.pine (or your own Pine) for on-chart RR visualization; inputs should reflect your 1:8–1:9 targets.
